brief_document.title: Differentiated auth error messages and header documentation
brief_document.purpose: All four auth failure modes return the identical 'namespace access denied' message. A developer cannot tell if they're missing the Authorization header, have a wrong token, are missing x-aion-subject, or have a namespace mismatch. Additionally, required headers (x-aion-subject, x-aion-namespaces) are undocumented.
brief_document.task: Differentiate auth error responses by failure mode. Add documentation for required headers in the hello-world README and a root-level API reference.

requirements.title: Differentiated auth error messages
requirements.spec: WHEN authentication fails, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a specific error message for each failure mode: missing Authorization header, invalid/expired bearer token, missing x-aion-subject header, namespace mismatch. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT return the same generic message for all four cases.
acceptance: No Authorization header → 'missing Authorization header with Bearer token'.
Invalid token → 'invalid or expired bearer token'.
Namespace mismatch → 'subject not authorized for namespace [name]'.
Each error includes a hint about what to fix.

requirements.title: Document required headers and API conventions
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL document all required headers (x-aion-subject, x-aion-namespaces, Authorization) in: (1) the hello-world README's curl examples with explanatory comments, and (2) a root-level docs/API.md reference. THE SYSTEM SHALL explain what each header does and what values are valid.
acceptance: hello-world README curl examples have inline comments explaining each header.
docs/API.md exists with a headers section listing all required/optional headers.
A developer reading only the README can construct valid requests.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the auth enforcement logic — only the error messages.
SHALL NOT add new headers — only document existing ones.
verification: Each auth failure mode returns a distinct, actionable error message.
